idea_text: Client onboarding blocker monitor for boutique design agencies

report.idea_text: Client onboarding blocker monitor for boutique design agencies
report.executive_summary: Boutique design agencies show direct onboarding pain, paid pilot intent, and repeated competitor weakness around client-side task completion.

evidence.title: Clients delay kickoff by missing onboarding files
evidence.summary: Direct pain: boutique design agencies lose launch momentum when clients miss onboarding files, access, and approvals.
evidence.quote: We cannot start until the client sends logo files, copy, DNS access, and approvals.

evidence.title: How do you make clients finish onboarding?
evidence.summary: Reachable community evidence: agency operators discuss client onboarding completion in r/agency.
evidence.icp_segment: agency owners managing recurring client onboarding

wtp_evidence.title: Agency owner would pay for blocker visibility
wtp_evidence.summary: Direct WTP: agency owner would pay $79/mo to reduce kickoff delays caused by missing client onboarding tasks.
wtp_evidence.quote: If it stops kickoff delays, $79 per month is easy to justify.

competitor_complaints.title: Asana tracks internal tasks but clients ignore onboarding
competitor_complaints.summary: Competitor complaint: Asana tracks internal tasks but lacks client-side completion pressure and launch-blocker visibility.
competitor_complaints.quote: Asana tells our team what is due, but clients still ignore their onboarding checklist.
competitor_complaints.workaround: manual email reminders and shared checklists

competitor_complaints.title: Client portals are too heavy for onboarding
competitor_complaints.summary: Competitor complaint: client portals are too heavy for simple onboarding and do not isolate launch blockers.

direct_competitors.name: Asana
direct_competitors.gap: Internal task tracking without client-side onboarding pressure.

direct_competitors.name: ClientPortalPro
direct_competitors.gap: Too heavy for lightweight onboarding blocker visibility.
